H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Strong-confidence anchor (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78). Strong right-skew (43/95 at 7-10). Qualitative confirms noticeable improvement (12/29), settle at 10 min (11/29). Music: 10 liked, 9 loved. AutoGluon prediction (6.859) lower than observed — may indicate early positive bias.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble effectively reduces overstimulation for most listeners, with strong improvements reported. It settles quickly (5-10 minutes) and works well in diverse contexts like commuting and caregiving.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Strong confidence (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73). Tight cluster at 6-9 with slight left tail. Settle delayed to 10 min (10/21). Music: 10 liked, 6 neutral. Rhythm split (7/14 calming, 7/14 balanced). Observes close to AutoGluon prediction (6.859).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall also reduces overstimulation effectively, though opinions on the music vary more. It takes a bit longer to take effect (around 10 minutes), but those who engage fully report noticeable benefits.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68). Tightest distribution in OS tier. Heavy sleep-activity skew (8/23 trying to sleep). Settle favors 5 min (7/23). Music: 11 liked, 5 neutral. Rhythm balanced (10/20).
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier is particularly effective for sleep-related overstimulation. Most settle within 5 minutes and find the rhythm balanced. Though smaller sample size, data aligns with other overstimulation protocols.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Strong-confidence protocol (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93) with tight right-tail distribution (26/67 at 8-10). Qualitative confirms moderate-to-significant improvement (20/23) with balanced/calming rhythm preferred. AutoGluon prediction (7.838) aligns closely with observed data. YouTube reach limited (7 views, no retention).
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow helps most listeners feel calmer, especially those dealing with stress. Most settle within 10 minutes and enjoy the balanced rhythm. It's effective for self-listeners and caregivers alike.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62). Moderate right-skew with clustered responses at 6-9. Qualitative shows mixed music opinions (9 liked, 5 neutral) and slower settle times (4/20 at ~20 min). Panel divided on significance of slight mean delta (vs L1/L3).
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage offers similar relief with slightly more varying experiences. Some find it takes longer to feel effects (up to 20 minutes), and music opinions vary more than Meadow. Still generally effective for stress reduction.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89). Qualitative indicates longer entrainment curve with 10/20 settling at ~10 min and 4/20 near-end. Music opinion split (8 liked, 5 neutral) with rhythm strongly calming (10/20). Slight mean improvement over L2 but higher variance.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley works well for stress but may take longer to settle (many report effects near the end). Rhythm is strongly calming, though music receives mixed reactions. Mean improvement matches other anxiety protocols.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70). Crisis protocol baseline aligns with expectations. 12/24 report significant improvement despite acute distress. Settle times split (8/24 at 5 min, 5/24 at 10 min). Music favorable (11 liked, 5 loved).
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor provides solid crisis relief despite initial distress. About half notice moderate-to-significant improvement, often within 5-10 minutes. Listeners generally appreciate the calming rhythm during acute episodes.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70). Pattern mirrors H-CR-L1 with similar distribution. Qualitative: faster music settlement (3/16 at 5 min) but more neutral opinions (6 neutral, 5 loved). Lower n but consistent crisis protocol profile.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor offers crisis relief with faster music settlement than Anchor but more neutral reactions to the sound. Works well for those already in distress, with moderate improvement reported across the board.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91). Highest variance in crisis tier with flat distribution. Fastest settle (7/19 immediate). Mixed music reception (7 liked, 4 not liked). Highest non-settle rate in crisis protocols.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon helps in crisis situations but shows the most varied responses. Some feel immediate relief, others need more time, and a few don't connect with it. Rhythm opinions are more mixed than other crisis protocols.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Strong confidence (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86). Strong right tail (25/54 at 7-10). Self-listener dominant (13/15). Rapid settle (5/15 immediate). Music: 10 liked, 3 loved. AutoGluon prediction (7.539) closely matches observed.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern promotes restful sleep for self-listeners, with noticeable improvements reported. It works relatively quickly (5 minutes or less) and receives strong approval for music and rhythm. One of the highest-rated sleep protocols.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55). Clinical listener skew unique in library (11 clinical/support vs 4 self). Settle at 10 min (7/15). Music neutral-heavy (6/15 neutral). Rhythm: 8 balanced but 3/15 'too busy'. Investigate population effects.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock helps diverse listeners including those in clinical settings fall asleep. While the music receives mixed reactions, many settle within 10 minutes. Slightly more report 'busy' rhythm perceptions than other sleep protocols.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68). Settle at 5 min (8/22). Music: 8 liked, 6 neutral. Rhythm: 9 balanced, 7 slightly busy. Slightly more spread than L2 but similar mean.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight supports sleep effectively, with most settling within 5 minutes. Music receives moderate approval, and rhythm is generally balanced. Consistent performer despite smaller sample size.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82). Rhythm conflict: 9/23 'slightly busy' despite 15/23 strong music approval. Settle favors 5 min (7/23) with 6/23 immediate. AutoGluon predicts lower-than-observed (7.728 vs 6.46).
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass supports focus and clarity with modest results. The rhythm feels slightly busy to many despite approving the music. Roughly split between immediate effects (some) and longer settle times (others).

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60). 10-min settle (9/17). Music neutral-heavy (6 neutral, 5 loved). Rhythm balanced (8/17). 0.73 mean jump from L1 warrants tier investigation.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint improves focus with better consistency than L1. Settles around 10 minutes and received mixed music reactions. Mean jump suggests improved design in L2 variant.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97). Bimodal: 10 scores at 10 but 7 at 6-7. 11/29 significant improvement (highest dataset). Settle: 15/29 at 10 min. 5/29 from YouTube — unique reach signal. AutoGluon prediction closely matches.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit stands out in focus protocols with highest mean score. Many report significant improvement, especially those using it while relaxing or doing chores. Strong music approval appears to drive outcomes.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73). Qualitative shows cautious improvement (8/21 slight). Settle split (6/21 immediate, 6/21 at 10 min). Music: 9 liked, 6 loved. Lower confidence reflects smaller n and left-skew distribution.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember supports creative flow with modest improvements for most listeners. Some notice effects immediately, others need about 10 minutes. Overall music approval is positive but not enthusiastic, suggesting entry-level positioning.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41). Tightest THRIVING distribution. Qualitative confirms strong music reception (6 loved, 6 liked) and 10-min settle (5/15). AutoGluon prediction supports consistent efficacy profile.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current helps creative flow with tighter consistency than L1. Listeners who engage report strong music appreciation and settle within 10 minutes. Suggests good design for dedicated creative sessions.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Developing with strong signal (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32). Tight std despite small n. High drift (0.586) but qualitative positive (9 liked, 7 neutral). 7/25 noticeable/significant improvement. Analytically distinct cluster (mean_sim=0.742).
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora shows promise for creative flow with small but tight outcomes. High drift flags evolving patterns, but music opinions remain positive. Early data suggests strong potential for focused creative work.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34). Slowest settle (9/23 at 20 min). High drift (0.565) despite strong outcomes. AutoGluon prediction lower (7.693) than observed. Therapy-session activity skew (8/23).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone supports balance with strong outcomes but slowest settle (up to 20 minutes). The music receives mixed reactions despite strong rhythm balance. High analytical drift flags evolving patterns.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03). Highest THRIVING variance. Settle at 5 min (6/16). Music: 9 liked. Rhythm split (7 busy, 7 balanced). High drift (0.637) and distribution spread suggest unaccounted subgroup.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis shows varied balance outcomes correlating with busy perception (some) and balanced (others). Music receives moderate approval but outcomes vary significantly. Flags possible population subgroups.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Developing (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73). Highest drift (0.557). Settle: 8/19 at 5 min with 4/19 non-settle (highest rate). Clustered with T-MB-L1/L2 (sim=0.778-0.844). Investigate phase duration relationship.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit helps balance with small but positive outcomes. Highest drift suggests evolving interpretation despite settle around 5 minutes. Unique but appears analytically related to other maintenance protocols.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94). Qualitative confirms moderate improvement (7/22) with care-giving activity (5/22). Music: 10 loved (highest), 6 neutral. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 for low zone.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze boosts mood and energy with solid outcomes for caregivers. Settles slowly (up to 20 minutes) but shows significant improvement reports. Listeners strongly love the music and balanced rhythm.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71). Significant improvement: 7/15 (highest ratio). Settle: 4/15 immediate. Activity: 2 trying to sleep, 2 therapy. Music: 7 liked, 6 loved. AutoGluon prediction aligns with observed.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise effectively improves mood and energy with strongest improvement reports (7/15). Settles quickly (immediate) for many and works well in therapy contexts. Music approval is strong.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Developing (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74). Lowest TRANSFORMING mean outside crisis. 6/25 significant improvement. Activity: 5 relaxing, 5 chores. Music: 11 liked, 7 loved (strongest approval). Rhythm: 9 very calming.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft underperforms for mood and energy but shows strong music approval. Settles around 5 minutes. High no-effect rate (5/25) suggests possible population mismatch or insufficient arc length.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01). Lowest pct_pos (45%). Settle at 5 min (9/17). 5/17 significant improvement. Activity: 4 studying. Music: 8 liked, 5 neutral. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation zone.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath motivates generally, but outcomes vary widely. Most settle within 5 minutes. Music receives moderate approval, and those needing motivation report significant help about a quarter of the time.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Developing (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63). Settle: 5/21 at 20 min (slowest). Music: 9 loved (highest ratio). Rhythm: 9 balanced, 6 slightly busy. Panel divided on significance of 0.15 delta vs L3.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail supports motivation but with developing data. Unique 20-minute settle pattern. Music receives strongest approval of any protocol, suggesting potential if design is adjusted for consistency.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Developing (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38). Highest spread in TRANSFORMING MOTIVATION. 8/17 moderate improvement. Activity: 6 trying to sleep. Music: 6 liked, 4 loved. Rhythm: 8 balanced, 4 slightly busy. Panel divided on reliability.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback motivates effectively but with high variance. Works well during relaxation and sleep activity types. Music approval is strong, and rhythm is generally balanced but busy for some.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62). Music strong: 9 loved, 9 liked. Rhythm: 9 balanced, 8 calming. Negative signal: 2/25 report worsening. AutoGluon prediction (7.538) higher than observed.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern aids mind clarity with solid music approval but shows two notable negative responses. Most settle within 10 minutes. Strong balance of calming and balanced rhythm perceptions.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Developing (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64). Highest calming ratio (12/20). Settle: 6/20 immediate. Music: 6 loved, 6 liked. AutoGluon prediction (7.538) aligns with developing observed data.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight similarly supports mind clarity with immediate and 10-minute settle times. Most strongly appreciate the calming rhythm. Early data suggests similar promise to L1.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Developing with concerning low signal (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45). Bimodal distribution [0,0,1,1,2,1,0,3,0,2,0] shows empty cores. Negative responses: 5/25 no effect. Music: 6 liked, 5 neutral. Rhythm: 6 calming, 4 busy. Panel consensus on population mismatch.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos has concerning results for mind clarity, with empty score distribution regions and multiple no-effect reports. High variance suggests either wrong population or flawed design. Listeners give mixed rhythm feedback.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The protocol library demonstrates solid baseline efficacy across all three series, with HEALING (mean=6.98) and THRIVING (mean=6.96) nearly identical and TRANSFORMING (mean=6.59) trailing modestly with higher variance.
• HEALING protocols show strongest overall performance with lowest variance (std=1.77) and highest confidence tiers
• THRIVING protocols display balanced efficacy with standout Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L3: 7.63 mean)
• TRANSFORMING exhibits expected high variability (std=1.87) with mixed performer patterns
• All three series show significant cross-bucket efficacy signals, particularly for ASCENT protocols
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series efficacy patterns align with design intentions while revealing unexpected overlaps.
• HEALING (DESCENT protocols): Highest mean (6.98) with Anxiety & Stress (H-AX: 7.22) and Sleep (7.19) as top performers
• THRIVING (HOLD protocols): Focused performance with T-FC-L3 (7.63) highest despite smaller n (35)
• TRANSFORMING (mixed entry): Lower mean (6.59) but X-ME protocols strongly cross-bucket recommended for acute states
• AutoGluon confirms DESCENT protocols outperform for acute intensity, while ASCENT protocols show untapped cross-utility
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Multiple divergent signals warrant investigation, with some protocols showing inconsistent data patterns.
• X-MC-L3: Bimodal distribution with extreme low mean (5.7, n=10) and highest std (2.45) — suggests population mismatch or ceiling effect
• T-MB-L3: Highest drift similarity (0.557) indicates analytical instability despite moderate outcomes (6.93 mean)
• T-FC-L1: 'Slightly busy' rhythm opinion recorded in 39% of responses (9/23) despite strong music approval
• Note: These anomalies may reflect genuine protocol limitations or data collection artifacts
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Convergent signals highlight specific protocols needing optimization or routing adjustments.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 requires redesign or population re-targeting — lowest mean (5.7) with bimodal responses
• T-MB-L1/T-MB-L2/T-MB-L3 cluster shows high drift (0.557-0.637) and variable outcomes — investigate phase duration
• T-FC-L3 outperforms peers (7.63 mean) but AutoGluon predicts cross-bucket utility to ASCENT protocols — extend hold phase
• H-SL-L2 shows inverted listener-type ratio (clinical:11 vs self:4) — verify clinical efficacy or routing adjustments
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals analytical instability in THRIVING and semantic clustering patterns.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 19/30 protocols show drift below 0.85 similarity, with THRIVING protocols (T-MB-L3: 0.557, T-CF-L3: 0.586) most affected
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-MB protocols form tight cluster (0.778-0.844 similarity), T-CF-L3 emerges as distinct (mean_sim=0.742)
• Confidence note: Drift reliable only for protocols above 0.85 similarity threshold; low run count (2-3) limits pattern stability

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow helps most listeners feel calmer, especially those dealing with stress. Most settle within 10 minutes and enjoy the balanced rhythm. It's effective for self-listeners and caregivers alike.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage offers similar relief with slightly more varying experiences. Some find it takes longer to feel effects (up to 20 minutes), and music opinions vary more than Meadow. Still generally effective for stress reduction.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley works well for stress but may take longer to settle (many report effects near the end). Rhythm is strongly calming, though music receives mixed reactions. Mean improvement matches other anxiety protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor provides solid crisis relief despite initial distress. About half notice moderate-to-significant improvement, often within 5-10 minutes. Listeners generally appreciate the calming rhythm during acute episodes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor offers crisis relief with faster music settlement than Anchor but more neutral reactions to the sound. Works well for those already in distress, with moderate improvement reported across the board.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon helps in crisis situations but shows the most varied responses. Some feel immediate relief, others need more time, and a few don't connect with it. Rhythm opinions are more mixed than other crisis protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble effectively reduces overstimulation for most listeners, with strong improvements reported. It settles quickly (5-10 minutes) and works well in diverse contexts like commuting and caregiving.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall also reduces overstimulation effectively, though opinions on the music vary more. It takes a bit longer to take effect (around 10 minutes), but those who engage fully report noticeable benefits.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier is particularly effective for sleep-related overstimulation. Most settle within 5 minutes and find the rhythm balanced. Though smaller sample size, data aligns with other overstimulation protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern promotes restful sleep for self-listeners, with noticeable improvements reported. It works relatively quickly (5 minutes or less) and receives strong approval for music and rhythm. One of the highest-rated sleep protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock helps diverse listeners including those in clinical settings fall asleep. While the music receives mixed reactions, many settle within 10 minutes. Slightly more report 'busy' rhythm perceptions than other sleep protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight supports sleep effectively, with most settling within 5 minutes. Music receives moderate approval, and rhythm is generally balanced. Consistent performer despite smaller sample size.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember supports creative flow with modest improvements for most listeners. Some notice effects immediately, others need about 10 minutes. Overall music approval is positive but not enthusiastic, suggesting entry-level positioning.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current helps creative flow with tighter consistency than L1. Listeners who engage report strong music appreciation and settle within 10 minutes. Suggests good design for dedicated creative sessions.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora shows promise for creative flow with small but tight outcomes. High drift flags evolving patterns, but music opinions remain positive. Early data suggests strong potential for focused creative work.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass supports focus and clarity with modest results. The rhythm feels slightly busy to many despite approving the music. Roughly split between immediate effects (some) and longer settle times (others).
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint improves focus with better consistency than L1. Settles around 10 minutes and received mixed music reactions. Mean jump suggests improved design in L2 variant.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit stands out in focus protocols with highest mean score. Many report significant improvement, especially those using it while relaxing or doing chores. Strong music approval appears to drive outcomes.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone supports balance with strong outcomes but slowest settle (up to 20 minutes). The music receives mixed reactions despite strong rhythm balance. High analytical drift flags evolving patterns.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis shows varied balance outcomes correlating with busy perception (some) and balanced (others). Music receives moderate approval but outcomes vary significantly. Flags possible population subgroups.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit helps balance with small but positive outcomes. Highest drift suggests evolving interpretation despite settle around 5 minutes. Unique but appears analytically related to other maintenance protocols.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern aids mind clarity with solid music approval but shows two notable negative responses. Most settle within 10 minutes. Strong balance of calming and balanced rhythm perceptions.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight similarly supports mind clarity with immediate and 10-minute settle times. Most strongly appreciate the calming rhythm. Early data suggests similar promise to L1.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos has concerning results for mind clarity, with empty score distribution regions and multiple no-effect reports. High variance suggests either wrong population or flawed design. Listeners give mixed rhythm feedback.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath motivates generally, but outcomes vary widely. Most settle within 5 minutes. Music receives moderate approval, and those needing motivation report significant help about a quarter of the time.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail supports motivation but with developing data. Unique 20-minute settle pattern. Music receives strongest approval of any protocol, suggesting potential if design is adjusted for consistency.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback motivates effectively but with high variance. Works well during relaxation and sleep activity types. Music approval is strong, and rhythm is generally balanced but busy for some.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze boosts mood and energy with solid outcomes for caregivers. Settles slowly (up to 20 minutes) but shows significant improvement reports. Listeners strongly love the music and balanced rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise effectively improves mood and energy with strongest improvement reports (7/15). Settles quickly (immediate) for many and works well in therapy contexts. Music approval is strong.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft underperforms for mood and energy but shows strong music approval. Settles around 5 minutes. High no-effect rate (5/25) suggests possible population mismatch or insufficient arc length.
